input.background: Patients with chronic renal failure undergoing haemodialysis were divided into groups with low or normal plasma heparin cofactor II (HC II) levels (Toulon et al., 1987). Five months later the acute effects of haemodialysis were studied by analysing plasma samples taken before and after haemodialysis. As dialysis increases total protein concentration in plasma, the ratio of HC II to protein was calculated, with the results shown in the following table.
input.question: Carry out a better analysis of these data.

output.answer: The correct way to compare the groups is by testing directly the difference between the changes in the two groups. A two sample t test gives t=0.16 on 22 degrees of freedom (P=0.88). The SDs are rather different, so that the assumptions of the t test may not be considered reasonable. A similar result is, however, obtained from a Mann-Whitney test (z=0.89, P=0.37). There is thus no evidence to support the idea that the groups differ.
